sourceMovie.title: The Way of the Dragon
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Way of the Dragon (1972) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Transporter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Way of the Dragon clicked for you, The Transporter (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Rush Hour
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Way of the Dragon clicked for you, Rush Hour (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Way of the Dragon?
faq.answer: Try The Transporter, Rush Hour, The Equalizer 3—each shares DNA with The Way of the Dragon while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Way of the Dragon?
faq.answer: The Way of the Dragon (1972) is best described as Action, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Way of the Dragon worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
